tweets.text:
Horrible news coming out about Alpha Futures. 

Unfortunately this is all too common for the prop industry. 

It’s also exactly why we built Breakout the way we did. 

Flexing huge payouts means nothing if they can be denied. 

Trust is paramount in this industry. 

We're backed by Kraken and are the safest option in a sketchy industry. 

Don’t fall prey to counterparty risk.
tweets.quoted_tweet_text:
Another major prop firm just rugged its own traders.

Approved payouts, gone. Funded accounts, closed. Called it a refund.

Sad part is this isn't surprising anymore.
It's becoming the norm.

That's why Breakout is built different.
Backed by @krakenfx, a tier one exchange.

thread_tweets.text: @pabloticker @trades_sage Kraken bought our company.
thread_tweets.full_text: @pabloticker @trades_sage Kraken bought our company.

thread_tweets.text:
We didn’t rush to the bottom on pricing, no massive discounts, we have really good abuse detection (cross-account hedging, fraud), and our plans have tighter drawdowns which allow for no shitty rules. 

Then we just stare at the data and adjust as necessary (eg we no longer offer a 2-step, it was subset gracefully without nuking any accounts or payouts).
thread_tweets.full_text:
We didn’t rush to the bottom on pricing, no massive discounts, we have really good abuse detection (cross-account hedging, fraud), and our plans have tighter drawdowns which allow for no shitty rules. 

Then we just stare at the data and adjust as necessary (eg we no longer offer a 2-step, it was subset gracefully without nuking any accounts or payouts).

thread_tweets.text:
You cannot completely eliminate counterparty risk in prop 

It’s not unit economics vs counterparty risk as separate categories; these things are interdependent and interact 

It’s a financial product and many of these blowups are mispricing the product which means you get adversely selected (the counterparty risk that exploits the mispricing)
thread_tweets.full_text:
You cannot completely eliminate counterparty risk in prop 

It’s not unit economics vs counterparty risk as separate categories; these things are interdependent and interact 

It’s a financial product and many of these blowups are mispricing the product which means you get adversely selected (the counterparty risk that exploits the mispricing)

tweets.full_text:
Horrible news coming out about Alpha Futures. 

Unfortunately this is all too common for the prop industry. 

It’s also exactly why we built Breakout the way we did. 

Flexing huge payouts means nothing if they can be denied. 

Trust is paramount in this industry. 

We're backed by Kraken and are the safest option in a sketchy industry. 

Don’t fall prey to counterparty risk.

@risksnowball Do it

@dehinker Stay tuned

@Steve1v9c Mayne at check out

@pabloticker @trades_sage Kraken bought our company.

We didn’t rush to the bottom on pricing, no massive discounts, we have really good abuse detection (cross-account hedging, fraud), and our plans have tighter drawdowns which allow for no shitty rules. 

Then we just stare at the data and adjust as necessary (eg we no longer offer a 2-step, it was subset gracefully without nuking any accounts or payouts).

You cannot completely eliminate counterparty risk in prop 

It’s not unit economics vs counterparty risk as separate categories; these things are interdependent and interact 

It’s a financial product and many of these blowups are mispricing the product which means you get adversely selected (the counterparty risk that exploits the mispricing)

tweets.text:
Strait of Hormuz over the last 24 hours: war on.

No visible inbound tankers in the last 24 hours.

Reports stated that Iran fired at two vessels today. There was an escort that saw tankers exit, ATARAKTOS, was one of the VLCCs that exited.

With no diplomatic resolution for the Oman lane, we will likely see more escalation throughout this week. 

I have included the video from @MarineTraffic and my inbound tanker list.

tweets.text:
that bangkok fire is something else, would not recommend watching that video

cus its literally 20 second of diagonal rocket engine style firing, throughout the whole restaurant and out of the door of the restaurant 

the main question is: where is the air entering at those speeds, if the exhaust is exiting a door at this speed?

scientifically it is really confusing how thats even possible for that intensity to last that long, you'd need a very high natural gas (assuming for cooking) or air (oxygen) flow rate, and where tf is either coming from in this rate? cat could believe if they had a gas tank

but for flame to be able to get into this restaurant, you'd need air to be able to also enter at that rate, this i why this fire is so mindblowing

you could get a very high flow rate if flame is going upwards due to low pressure gas post combustion, eg. in from the windows, up a chimney, but this one is a puzzle, would love to read the investigation later
